findings.title: Any-to-Any Accept Rules
findings.detail: Rules accepting traffic from any source to any destination bypass network segmentation.
findings.recommendation: Replace with least-privilege rules specifying explicit sources, destinations, and services.

findings.title: PPTP VPN Enabled — Protocol is Cryptographically Broken
findings.detail: PPTP uses RC4-40/128 and MS-CHAPv2, both completely broken; handshakes crackable in under 24 hours.
findings.recommendation: Migrate all PPTP tunnels to IKEv2/IPSec or OpenVPN with AES-256-GCM.

findings.title: WAN-Zone Unrestricted Source Networks
findings.detail: Rules accept traffic sourced from WAN without restricting source network to specific IPs.
findings.recommendation: Restrict inbound rules to known CIDR ranges; enable IPS/AV policies on WAN-facing rules.

findings.title: Insecure/Legacy Service Protocols
findings.detail: Firewall rules explicitly permit Telnet (port 23), FTP (port 21), TFTP (port 69), and SNMPv1/v2.
findings.recommendation: Replace with SSH, SFTP/FTPS, and SNMPv3 with authentication and privacy (authPriv).

findings.title: Weak Encryption Algorithms in IPSec Policies
findings.detail: DES, 3DES, and NULL encryption are configured in IPSec phase proposals.
findings.recommendation: Use AES-128-GCM or AES-256-GCM only. Remove all DES, 3DES, and NULL proposals.

findings.title: SSL VPN Allows Deprecated TLS Versions
findings.detail: TLS 1.0/1.1 and SSL 3.0 are enabled, exposing connections to POODLE, BEAST, and CRIME attacks.
findings.recommendation: Enforce TLS 1.2 minimum; prefer TLS 1.3. Disable SSL 3.0, TLS 1.0, and TLS 1.1.

findings.title: Accept Rules with Logging Disabled
findings.detail: Traffic permitted by these rules is not logged, making forensic investigation impossible after an incident.
findings.recommendation: Enable logging on all accept rules. Forward logs to a central SIEM or syslog server.

findings.title: Disabled DROP/DENY Rules
findings.detail: Disabled deny rules remove a safety net; traffic may fall through to permissive default policy.
findings.recommendation: Re-enable disabled deny rules or delete them with documented business justification.
